column1: 2004-06-28‚ÄéI had an  EXTREMELY  bad family reunion this year. Apparently
column2: my aunt and uncle's nanny came to work with the stomach flu the day before they left to go to the reunion and got my year-old cousin sick. When they arrived
column3: my cousin got his 2 year old sister sick. In addition
column4: they stayed in the same cabin as my family. They got my family sick along with my grandparents
column5: and about 15 other family members sick. Everyone had vomited at least once
column6: including myself. My grandfather got it so bad that he passed out and we had to call for an ambulance. My cousins had to go to the hospital because of dehydration. It was  definately  a memorable experience.

column1: 2004-06-06‚Äé"I saw Troy on Friday. It was a very good movie
column2: but it had some very odd moments. One such moment was when the two giant armies are in the middle of fighting
column3: and when the prince of troy is fighting the greatest fighter of the greek army. When the prince kills Achilles (greatest fighter)
column4: he slits his throat and all of the soldiers stop to watch. When the prince pulls off the helm of Achilles
column5: he finds out that the person he was fighting was actually Achilles's young cousin. When the prince saw this
column6: he said to the general of the other army ""That's enough for today"" and both armies went back to their bases. I was left scratching my head at how both armies would go back to their bases in the middle of a freaking WAR!!!"

2004-07-04‚ÄéMy town's fireworks were very well done this year. However
column2: they seemed to have miscalculated where the ash from the fireworks would fall. Everyone in the area I was sitting in was bombarded with ash. I myself got hit with an uncountable number of quarter-sized pieces. Some landed while still on fire and nearly caused some fires. Hopefully next year they will think about where they are aiming the fireworks more carefully.
